stem: According to the chapter, what is the goal of an ethically sound sales process?

options.A: To close the sale as quickly as possible
options.B: To maximise the commission earned by the representative
options.C: Not to sell a product, but to inform the client why the product is in his best interest
options.D: To meet the financial institution's quarterly sales targets

explanation: 教材 1.3(c) 明确:符合道德的销售流程目标不是把产品卖出去,而是告知客户该产品为何符合其最佳利益。其余选项均以卖出/佣金/指标为先,与该原则相悖。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.20 — s.1.3(c)

stem: Which of the following is NOT one of the three ethical pitfalls in the marketing and sales process?

options.B: Failure to recommend and sell only suitable products
options.C: Failure to recognise and disclose relevant conflicts of interest
options.D: Charging a fee for financial advisory services

explanation: 教材 2.1 列出的三大陷阱为虚假陈述、未只推荐适当产品、未识别并披露利益冲突。就咨询服务收费本身并非教材所列陷阱。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.20 — s.2.1

stem: In the sale of a life insurance policy, who bears the risk on the non-guaranteed elements of the policy?
explanation: 教材 1.3(e) 指出,人寿保单中客户承担非保证部分的风险,而保险人承担保证部分的风险。代表须清楚向客户说明这一点。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.20 — s.1.3(e)

stem: Which statement best captures the difference between misrepresentation and fraud?

options.A: Fraud is always unintentional, while misrepresentation is always deliberate
options.B: Fraud is an intentional act of deception, while misrepresentation can be an unintentional omission or inadvertent misstatement
options.C: Misrepresentation is a criminal offence while fraud is only a civil matter
options.D: There is no difference; the two terms are interchangeable

explanation: 教材 3.4–3.5 指出,欺诈是故意的欺骗行为;而虚假陈述可以是无意的遗漏或疏忽的失实陈述,即可为 (a) 无意的或 (b) 遗漏行为。A 颠倒了概念,C/D 与教材不符。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.20 — s.3.4–3.5

stem: 'Lying by omission', where an important fact is left out in order to foster a misconception, is also known as:

options.D: An act of negligence per se

explanation: 教材 3.10 指出,lying by omission 又称 continuing misrepresentation(续行虚假陈述),包括未纠正既存误解。Lying by commission 则是把不实/半真陈述当事实传达(3.11)。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.20 — s.3.10–3.11

stem: In Singapore, from which sources is the law of misrepresentation derived?

options.B: Both the common law and the Misrepresentation Act (1967)
options.D: The Penal Code and the Securities and Futures Act

explanation: 教材 3.3 明确,在新加坡虚假陈述法源自普通法与《虚假陈述法 Misrepresentation Act (1967)》两者。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.20 — s.3.3

stem: Under Holley's continuum, which disclosure strategy corresponds to the 'caveat emptor' (buyer beware) rule?
explanation: 教材 4.4 指出,最低信息规则(Minimal Information Rule)即 'caveat emptor'(买方自负);修订最低信息规则则为 'caveat emptor plus'。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.20 — s.4.3(a)–4.4

stem: The chapter suggests that the best disclosure strategy for a financial services professional is to really 'know' his client and apply which approach?

options.C: The strict Maximal Information Rule in all cases

explanation: 教材 4.15 建议采用 'Mutual Benefit Rule Plus' 策略——专业人士须具备足够知识、能力与专业,帮助客户在可选项中做出最佳选择。最大信息规则被认为对代表过于苛刻(4.14)。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.20 — s.4.14–4.15

stem: Regulation requires FA corporations and their representatives to have a reasonable basis for any recommendation of an investment product. This requirement is found in:

options.A: Section 27(1) of the FAA and the Misrepresentation Act 1967
options.C: Section 36 of the Financial Advisers Act 2001 and FAA Notice No. 16

explanation: 教材 5.6 指出,合理依据要求源自《财务顾问法 FAA 2001》第 36 条 及 FAA Notice No: 16。FAA-N03(选项 B)是关于利益冲突披露的;Reg 21 关于 'independent' 称谓。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.20 — s.5.6 / FAA s.36, FAA Notice 16

stem: Which MAS Notice requires a financial adviser to disclose in writing any actual or potential conflict of interest arising from its connection with a product provider?
explanation: 教材 6.4 引用 FAA Notice FAA-N03,要求财务顾问就与任何产品提供方关联引起的实际或潜在利益冲突,以书面形式向客户披露。FAA Notice 16 是关于推荐合理依据的(适当性)。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.20 — s.6.4 / FAA-N03

stem: A bank's representative is proposing a financial product to a client who has a loan while also holding investments and fixed deposits with the same bank. What is the ethical course of action?

options.A: Say nothing, since the loan and the product are unrelated
options.B: Highlight the potential conflict of interest, because on default the bank may use the assets to off-set the loan
options.C: Recommend the client move his deposits to another bank first
options.D: Refuse to serve the client because of the conflict

explanation: 教材 6.15(例一)指出,因贷款违约时银行有权动用客户资产抵销贷款,故代表提议产品时合乎道德的做法是点明这一潜在利益冲突,而非隐瞒或拒绝服务。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.20 — s.6.15 (Example 1)

stem: Which of the following is one of the three pitfalls that can occur within the marketing process?

options.B: Using scare tactics that draw attention to 'doomsday examples'
options.D: Disclosing referral fees to the client

explanation: 教材 7.4 列出三类营销陷阱:用材料虚假呈现能力/资历、使用恐吓策略(scare tactics,如 doomsday examples)、设定不切实际的期望。其余选项是合乎道德的做法,非陷阱。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.20 — s.7.4 / s.7.13–7.15

stem: Peter used workshops to urge clients to surrender all their whole life policies and buy term insurance plus investments, without mentioning the re-setting of the incontestability clause, the non-guaranteed returns of the new funds, or the lack of a non-forfeiture clause on term insurance. Peter's conduct is BEST described as:

options.A: Ethical, because he helped clients restructure their policies
options.B: Ethical, because clients made their own decisions
options.C: Unethical, because he marketed the new products using only selective information
options.D: Acceptable, because term insurance is cheaper than whole life

explanation: 教材 7.32(例一)指出,Peter 仅以选择性信息营销新产品、隐瞒不可争议条款重置、非保证回报与缺乏不丧失价值条款等关键事实,因而行为不道德。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.20 — s.7.32 (Example 1)

stem: According to the chapter, what is the key distinction between marketing and selling?

options.A: Marketing converts the product into cash, while selling researches client needs
options.B: Marketing emphasises client satisfaction (meeting needs and wants), while selling is the action of converting the product or service into cash and emphasises the product
options.C: Marketing and selling are identical activities
options.D: Selling is regulated by MAS, while marketing is not regulated at all

explanation: 教材 7.1–7.2 指出,营销关乎满足客户需要与想要、强调客户满意;而销售是把产品/服务变现的行为、强调产品本身。A 颠倒了二者,C/D 与教材不符。
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.20 — s.7.1–7.2
